name: Chromate sensor (ChrR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects toxic hexavalent chromium (chromate) in industrial water. The ChrR chromate-responsive regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the robust GRAS industrial chassis Corynebacterium glutamicum.

input.detects: Hexavalent chromium in industrial effluent and water
input.operatingRange: ChrR/chr operon responds across µM chromate

sensing.sensorMechanism: ChrR/ChrB regulators of chromate-resistance operons respond to chromate and switch the chr promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Cr(VI) -> ChrR switches Pchr -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Pair an inverter for turn-on.

parts.name: dCas9
parts.source: Cleto et al. 2016 (CRISPRi in C. glutamicum)

performance.notes: ChrR chromate sensing and C. glutamicum CRISPRi are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. C. glutamicum tolerates harsh industrial samples.

safety.notes: GRAS, robust at high cell density for industrial monitoring.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the reporter promoter; check C. glutamicum off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: ChrR + Pchr -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + Ptuf -> sfGFP.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add effluent sample + chromate standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining a ChrR chromate-sensing module with C. glutamicum CRISPRi
partsSources: Cleto et al. 2016, ACS Synth Biol (CRISPRi in C. glutamicum)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
